setting.note: The wartime Budapest of a fifteen-year-old boy who is marked, taken and deported in the summer of 1944. The map holds the ordinary points of his last free weeks, a Pest flat, an apprentice job across the river at Csepel, and the brickyard on the northern edge where a stopped bus ends the ordinary. From there the novel travels out of the frame to the camps and, at the last, returns to a city that has changed while he was gone.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Imre Kertész, first published in Hungarian as Sorstalanság, 1975. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Fatelessness, Imre Kertész (1975). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: A plain, sober grey, chosen to sit quietly against the subject rather than to decorate it. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
